title: clinopodium gracile var. minimum
intro: Clinopodium gracile var. minimum, also known as lesser calamintha or small-stemmed clinopodium, is a small perennial herb that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. Clinopodium gracile var. minimum has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as colds and coughs, and to treat wounds and skin infections. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy, allowing for a variety of herbal preparations to be made from this versatile herb.
